name: The Calyxis Reclaimer
languages: understands Common and Modron but can't speak
resist: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks

trait.name: Colossal Vehicle
entries: The Calyxis Reclaimer is a three-car fortress platform. It is best treated as a moving set-piece with targetable subsystems, crew stations, and compartment fighting rather than as a stationary monster.

trait.name: Damage Threshold
entries: The Calyxis Reclaimer has a damage threshold of 15. If it would take 14 damage or less from a single source, it takes no damage instead.

trait.name: Siege Engine
entries: The Calyxis Reclaimer deals double damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Unstoppable Advance
entries: The Calyxis Reclaimer is not slowed by difficult terrain made of sand, scree, or shallow rubble.

trait.name: Crew Dependencies
entries: Its claws require Heliox Arm Operators to function, its harpoon system requires a Heliox Harpoon Gunner, and steering shifts work best while a Heliox Engine Overseer remains active at the control station.

action.name: Titan Drill (Recharge 5-6)
entries: Each creature or vehicle in a 15-foot line directly in front of the Calyxis Reclaimer must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking 33 ({@damage 6d10}) piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. A target that fails is also restrained (escape DC 18) as the drill catches or pins it.

action.name: Gripper Claws
entries: The Calyxis Reclaimer makes two gripper claw attacks.

action.name: Gripper Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 18). A grappled target is restrained.

action.name: Harpoon Shot
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 11} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}25 ({@damage 4d10 + 3}) piercing damage, and the target is tethered until it escapes with a DC 18 Strength check.

legendary.name: Rockfield Advance
entries: At initiative count 20, the Calyxis Reclaimer surges 60 feet forward. The DM can spawn 1d4 + 1 incoming rocks across six battlefield columns. Creatures or vehicles struck by rocks typically make Dexterity saves against bludgeoning damage, forced movement, or crash effects as appropriate to the encounter map.

legendary.name: Column Shift
entries: At initiative count 20, the Calyxis Reclaimer may shift 1 battlefield column left or right. Creatures or vehicles caught beneath or beside the shift must usually make a Dexterity saving throw or take crushing damage.

legendary.name: Runaway Engine
entries: If the engine crew are defeated but the Reclaimer is not disabled, it continues to move forward as a runaway fortress until its engine or steering systems are sabotaged.
